conflicts.title: Main CSV vs Team CSV Schema Mismatch
conflicts.description: Main CSV has 9 columns including separate 'team' field, while Team CSV has 8 columns with team embedded in 'code' field.
conflicts.impact: Cannot directly join or union these files without transformation

proposed_test.name: schema_normalization_validation
proposed_test.description: Extract team ID from Team CSV 'code' field and verify matches Main CSV 'team' field

resolution.details: Use Main CSV schema as canonical; transform Team CSV on ingestion

conflicts.title: GK CSV Contains Non-Subset Action Types
conflicts.description: GK CSV is expected to be a filtered subset of Main CSV but contains 36 action codes not present in Main CSV.

examples: 1. Mattia Perin (1141014) - İsabetli Orta Mesafeli Şutlar

conflicts.impact: GK-specific metrics cannot be derived from Main CSV alone

proposed_test.name: gk_event_taxonomy_mapping
proposed_test.description: Map GK-only events to their parent categories in Main CSV
proposed_test.implementation: For each GK-only action, identify equivalent or parent action in Main taxonomy
proposed_test.expected_result: 100% GK actions mappable to Main taxonomy OR explicit new category definition

resolution.details: Formally define goalkeeper event taxonomy as extension to main taxonomy

conflicts.title: Exact Duplicate Events at Single Timestamp
conflicts.description: 6 exact duplicate records found at timestamp 6175.33s (ET Half 1, ~103 min)
conflicts.impact: Metric inflation by 2x for affected events if not deduplicated

proposed_test.name: duplicate_source_analysis
proposed_test.description: Determine if duplicates are data provider error or collection artifact
proposed_test.implementation: Query provider metadata for affected timestamp; check video frame alignment
proposed_test.expected_result: Root cause identification: provider bug vs multiple observation

resolution.details: Either apply deduplication with audit trail OR block entire match until clarified

conflicts.title: Team CSV Has 682 More Events Than Main CSV
conflicts.description: Team-level aggregation file contains more events than player-level file, contrary to expectation.
conflicts.impact: Unclear aggregation relationship; cannot reconcile team totals from player events

proposed_test.name: aggregation_reconciliation
proposed_test.description: For each timestamp, verify sum(player_events) == team_events
proposed_test.implementation: GROUP BY start, end; COUNT player vs team events; identify discrepancies
proposed_test.expected_result: Clear aggregation rule OR identification of team-only event types

resolution.details: Document exact rules for player→team aggregation

conflicts.title: 13 Action Types Exclusive to Each File
conflicts.description: Main CSV and Team CSV each have 13 unique action types not present in the other.

main_only: Şutla Biten Duran Toplarda Yer Alma

conflicts.impact: Cannot compute certain metrics from a single file

proposed_test.name: action_taxonomy_completeness
proposed_test.description: Build unified action taxonomy covering both files
proposed_test.implementation: UNION all unique actions; classify as PLAYER_ONLY, TEAM_ONLY, or COMMON
proposed_test.expected_result: Complete taxonomy document with 74+ action definitions

resolution.details: Build canonical action ontology for HPFA ingestion layer

conflicts.title: Non-Zero Time Base Offset
conflicts.description: Match events start at 5.24 seconds instead of 0, indicating data collection delay or offset.
conflicts.impact: All time-based calculations must account for offset

proposed_test.name: time_base_consistency
proposed_test.description: Verify offset is constant across all events and files
proposed_test.implementation: MIN(start) across all files should be identical

resolution.details: Subtract 5.24s in ingestion layer; document as provider-specific

conflicts.title: Boundary Coordinate Accumulation
conflicts.description: 109 events have pos_y = 0.0 exactly, suggesting boundary/edge classification.
conflicts.impact: May affect spatial analysis; touchline events need special handling

proposed_test.name: edge_event_classification
proposed_test.description: Analyze action types for pos_y=0 events
proposed_test.implementation: GROUP BY action WHERE pos_y = 0; verify expected set piece types
proposed_test.expected_result: Edge events are throw-ins, corners, or goal kicks

resolution.details: Add boolean edge_event column; exclude from spatial metrics if needed

counter_scenarios.title: Duplicates Are Intentional Multi-Observation
counter_scenarios.description: The 6 duplicate events at t=6175.33 might represent legitimate multiple observations of the same physical moment from different analytical perspectives.
counter_scenarios.hypothesis: Provider intentionally records same action under multiple taxonomic categories (e.g., 'Başarılı dripling' is ALSO '3. Bölgede Başarılı Dripling').
evidence_for: Both action types describe successful dribbles
Same timestamp indicates same physical event
evidence_against: Exact code duplication suggests copy-paste error
No documentation of multi-category tagging policy
Other timestamps don't show this pattern consistently

test_to_break.name: pattern_consistency_check
test_to_break.description: Search for similar multi-tagging at other timestamps
test_to_break.breaking_condition: If < 5% of timestamps show this pattern, likely error; if > 20%, likely intentional

counter_scenarios.conclusion_if_true: Keep both records but add parent-child relationship in metadata

counter_scenarios.title: GK File Is Enhanced View, Not Subset
counter_scenarios.description: GK CSV might be intentionally enriched with goalkeeper-specific metrics not tracked for outfield players.
counter_scenarios.hypothesis: Provider generates separate goalkeeper reports with specialized action types (shot classification, save types) that don't apply to outfield players.
evidence_for: GK-only actions are goalkeeper-specific (shot types, save types)
2 goalkeepers in XLSX GK file have different metrics than outfield
Common in sports analytics to have positional specialization
evidence_against: No documentation indicates separate extraction logic
Some GK events should appear in Main (e.g., saves)

test_to_break.name: gk_action_outfield_applicability
test_to_break.description: Check if any GK-only action types could apply to outfield players
test_to_break.breaking_condition: If any outfield player in Main has GK-only action type → File is incomplete, not specialized

counter_scenarios.conclusion_if_true: Treat GK CSV as supplementary data source; define merge logic
counter_scenarios.conclusion_if_false: GK CSV has extraction error; flag for provider correction

break_conditions.name: Duplicate Pattern Consistency
break_conditions.metric: percentage of timestamps with multi-category tagging

break_conditions.name: GK Action Outfield Applicability
break_conditions.metric: count of outfield players with GK-only actions
break_conditions.action_if_breaks: Flag GK CSV as incomplete extraction

break_conditions.name: Golden Reference Deviation
break_conditions.metric: deviation from independently verified match data
break_conditions.action_if_breaks: System is theoretical, not operationally validated
